root_text.sn56.38:1.1: “Yāvakīvañca, bhikkhave, candimasūriyā loke nuppajjanti, neva tāva mahato ālokassa pātubhāvo hoti mahato obhāsassa.
root_text.sn56.38:1.3: Neva tāva rattindivā paññāyanti, na māsaddhamāsā paññāyanti, na utusaṁvaccharā paññāyanti.
root_text.sn56.38:2.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, candimasūriyā loke uppajjanti, atha mahato ālokassa pātubhāvo hoti mahato obhāsassa.
root_text.sn56.38:2.2: Neva andhakāratamaṁ tadā hoti na andhakāratimisā.
root_text.sn56.38:2.3: Atha rattindivā paññāyanti, māsaddhamāsā paññāyanti, utusaṁvaccharā paññāyanti.
root_text.sn56.38:2.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, yāvakīvañca tathāgato loke nuppajjati arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, neva tāva mahato ālokassa pātubhāvo hoti mahato obhāsassa.
root_text.sn56.38:2.6: Neva tāva catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ ācikkhaṇā hoti desanā paññāpanā paṭṭhapanā vivaraṇā vibhajanā uttānīkammaṁ.
root_text.sn56.38:3.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, tathāgato loke uppajjati arahaṁ sammāsambuddho, atha mahato ālokassa pātubhāvo hoti mahato obhāsassa.
root_text.sn56.38:3.2: Neva andhatamaṁ tadā hoti na andhakāratimisā.
root_text.sn56.38:3.3: Atha kho catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ ācikkhaṇā hoti desanā paññāpanā paṭṭhapanā vivaraṇā vibhajanā uttānīkammaṁ.
root_text.sn56.38:4.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.38:1.1: “Mendicants, as long as the moon and the sun don’t arise in the world, no great light or great radiance appears.
translation_text.sn56.38:1.3: Day and night aren’t found, nor months and fortnights, nor seasons and years.
translation_text.sn56.38:2.1: But when the moon and the sun arise in the world, a great light, a great radiance appears.
translation_text.sn56.38:2.3: Day and night are found, and months and fortnights, and seasons and years.
translation_text.sn56.38:2.4: In the same way, as long as the Realized One doesn’t arise in the world, no great light or great radiance appears.
translation_text.sn56.38:2.6: There’s no explanation of the four noble truths, no teaching, advocating, establishing, clarifying, analyzing, and revealing of them.
translation_text.sn56.38:3.1: But when the Realized One arises in the world, a great light, a great radiance appears.
translation_text.sn56.38:3.3: Then there’s the explanation of the four noble truths, the teaching, advocating, establishing, clarifying, analyzing, and revealing of them.
translation_text.sn56.38:3.5: The noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.38:4.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
